title: Apache Iceberg & Delta Lake Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of modern table formats, ACID guarantees, time travel, and the data lakehouse pattern.

questions.question: What fundamental limitation of raw Parquet files on S3 do table formats like Iceberg and Delta Lake solve?

options.text: Parquet files cannot store nested data types like arrays and structs
Raw Parquet provides no ACID guarantees — concurrent reads/writes cause partial data reads, and there's no way to atomically update or delete individual rows
Parquet's columnar compression is too slow for real-time analytics
S3 has a 5GB file size limit that Parquet files frequently exceed

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'time travel' in Apache Iceberg?

options.text: It optimizes queries by predictively caching data expected to be queried in the future
It allows querying the table as it existed at any past snapshot or timestamp — enabling audit, debugging bad pipeline writes, and regulatory data reconstruction
It replicates data to multiple geographical regions automatically for disaster recovery
It delays write operations to batch them for better performance

questions.question: Why do data engineers run regular 'compaction' jobs against Iceberg or Delta Lake tables?

options.text: Compaction re-encrypts table data each night to maintain security compliance
Streaming pipeline micro-batches create thousands of small Parquet files over time — compaction merges them into larger optimally-sized files, significantly improving query scan performance
Compaction validates the Parquet schema for consistency across all partitions
Compaction is required to make time travel features available on older data versions

questions.question: In which scenario would you choose Apache Iceberg over Delta Lake?

options.text: When your organization is entirely on Databricks and uses Unity Catalog for governance
When you need a multi-engine open stack where Trino, Apache Flink, AWS Athena, and Spark all read the same tables without vendor lock-in
When you need ACID transactions — Delta Lake does not support them
When deploying on Azure, since Iceberg has native Azure Synapse integration

questions.question: What happens to deleted rows' underlying Parquet files after a DELETE operation in Iceberg if you never run `expire_snapshots`?

options.text: They are immediately removed from S3 because the metadata no longer references them
They remain on S3 indefinitely — Iceberg only removes logically from the snapshot metadata. Expire snapshots + vacuum must be run explicitly to reclaim physical storage
They are moved to S3 Glacier for cold storage automatically
They are retained for exactly 30 days and then auto-deleted by the catalog
